name: Stability and consistency imply convergence

statement_latex:
```latex
\begin{lemma}[515A]
Assume $h \le h_0$, with $h_0 L \|A\|_\infty < 1$. Define $\phi \in \mathbb{R}^s$ by
\[
\sum_{j=1}^s (\delta_{ij} - h_0 L|a_{ij}|)\phi_j = \frac12 c_i^2 + \sum_{j=1}^s |a_{ij}c_j|.
\]
Let $y_i^{[n-1]} = u_i y(x_{n-1}) + v_i h y'(x_{n-1})$, $y_i^{[n]} = u_i y(x_n) + v_i h y'(x_n)$ for $i=1,\dots,r$, and $\widehat Y_i = y(x_{n-1}+hc_i)$ for $i=1,\dots,s$, where $c = A\mathbf{1} + Uv$. Let $\widehat Y_i^\ast$ be the value computed exactly from $y^{[n-1]}$. Assume $f$ is Lipschitz with constant $L$ and the exact solution satisfies $\|y(x)\| \le M$, $\|y'(x)\| \le LM$. Then
\[
\Bigl\| \widehat Y_i - h\sum_{j=1}^s a_{ij} f(\widehat Y_j) - \sum_{j=1}^r U_{ij} y_j^{[n-1]} \Bigr\|
\le h^2 L^2 M \Bigl( \frac12 c_i^2 + \sum_{j=1}^s |a_{ij}c_j| \Bigr),
\]
\[
\Bigl\| y_i^{[n]} - h\sum_{j=1}^s b_{ij} f(\widehat Y_j) - \sum_{j=1}^r V_{ij} y_j^{[n-1]} \Bigr\|
\le h^2 L^2 M \Bigl( \frac12 |u_i| + |v_i| + \sum_{j=1}^s |b_{ij}c_j| \Bigr).
\]
\end{lemma}
```

context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
Consider a general linear method for an ODE $y' = f(y)$ with step size $h \in (0, h_0]$. Let $L$ be the Lipschitz constant for $f$, and assume $\|y(x)\| \le M$, $\|f(y)\| \le LM$ for $y$ in a closed set $S$ containing the trajectory. The method uses $s$ internal stages $Y_i$ and $r$ input/output approximations combining $y(x)$ and $hy'(x)$. The coefficients are: $A \in \mathbb{R}^{s \times s}$, $U \in \mathbb{R}^{r \times s}$, $v \in \mathbb{R}^{r}$, and stage abscissae $c = A\mathbf{1} + U v$. Input approximations use coefficients $u_i$, $v_i$ for $i=1,\dots,r$.

variables.name: h0
variables.definition: upper bound on step size, h ∈ (0, h0]

variables.name: L
variables.definition: Lipschitz constant for f, also bounds derivative of solution

variables.name: A
variables.definition: s × s matrix of coefficients in the general linear method

variables.name: U
variables.definition: r × s matrix of coefficients in the general linear method

variables.name: v
variables.definition: r-vector of coefficients in the general linear method

variables.name: c
variables.definition: s-vector of stage abscissae, c = A1 + U v

variables.name: y(x)
variables.definition: exact solution of the initial value problem

variables.name: f
variables.definition: function defining the ODE, satisfies Lipschitz condition with constant L

variables.name: M
variables.definition: bound such that ||y(x)|| ≤ M and ||f(y)|| ≤ LM

variables.name: S
variables.definition: closed set in R^N containing all trajectory values of y(x)

preamble: The lemma analyzes the local truncation error for a general linear method applied to an ODE. The method uses internal stages Yi and input/output approximations combining y(x) and hy'(x). The problem assumes a Lipschitz condition on f and boundedness of the solution and its derivative.

dependencies.name: Lipschitz condition in its second variable
